Set up a new Python environment or Colab notebook to isolate the test case
Check the current installed version of Keras by running `pip show keras` or `import keras; print(keras.__version__)`
Import TensorFlow and Keras modules with `import tensorflow as tf` and `from tensorflow import keras` to confirm availability
Attempt to import `data_utils` from `keras.utils` with `from keras.utils import data_utils` and observe whether it raises an ImportError
Install the latest Keras version available by running `pip install --upgrade keras` to reproduce the bug with the latest release
Run the import line `from keras.utils import data_utils` again to confirm the ImportError occurs consistently with the latest Keras version
Clone or download the Fine-tuning Stable Diffusion example code from https://keras.io/examples/generative/finetune_stable_diffusion/
In the example code environment, execute the example scripts in a fresh Colab session with the latest Keras version installed
Observe the failure traceback showing the ImportError: cannot import name 'data_utils' from 'keras.utils'
Record the full traceback and error message exactly as shown in the bug report
Uninstall the current Keras version with `pip uninstall keras -y`
Install Keras version 2.12.0 explicitly by running `pip install keras==2.12.0`
Re-run the Fine-tuning Stable Diffusion example script in the same environment
Verify that the import error does not occur and the example runs successfully without import errors
Assert that the import error is resolved only when Keras version 2.12.0 is installed
Document the Keras version requirement by printing `keras.__version__` inside the example script before the import that fails
Conclude that the ImportError is reproducible only with Keras versions newer than 2.12.0 due to removal or relocation of `data_utils` in `keras.utils`
